unit_of_analysis: social-media-influenced donation channel and campaign summary
outcome_metric: Donation revenue and donor quality from existing marketing attribution tables
evidence_strength: Directional guidance derived from current donation channel and campaign analysis. Useful for planning content strategy now, but not yet a true post-level causal model.
directional_findings: Social media-attributed giving already accounts for 43 donations totaling PHP 43,590.98 from 29 unique donors, so the channel is materially important enough to prioritize.

Donors acquired via social media show higher average lifetime value (PHP 5,610.78) than the overall donor base (PHP 4,185.11), a directional lift of about 34.1%, though the current comparison is not statistically significant.

Named campaigns are associated with about 7.9% higher donation amounts than non-campaign giving, which suggests themed campaign bursts may outperform sporadic standalone posts even though the estimate is not yet significant.

The SocialMedia channel shows an estimated +14.6% effect on donation amounts versus baseline, which is directional evidence to keep social content tied to fundraising outcomes and not just engagement.

recommendations.title: Keep social posts donor-focused
recommendations.detail: Because social media already contributes meaningful donation value and 48.8% of those gifts are recurring, the chatbot should favor posts with a clear fundraising purpose instead of optimizing only for likes or awareness.

recommendations.title: Generate content in campaign bursts
recommendations.detail: Because named campaigns show a positive estimated lift relative to no-campaign giving, the chatbot should package ideas as short themed campaigns rather than unrelated one-off posts.

recommendations.title: Use specific asks with mission clarity
recommendations.detail: Current data is strongest at the channel and campaign level, so the chatbot should emphasize concrete donation CTAs, trust-building language, and clear connection to impact while avoiding overconfident claims about exact timing or platform superiority.

data_gaps: Current evidence is channel-level and campaign-level, not individual post-level.
Platform-specific comparisons such as Facebook versus Instagram are not directly observed in the current data.
Timing effects, content-theme effects, and CTA-level effects are still directional hypotheses until post-level attribution becomes available.
